description: The state or period of being young; a time of growth, potential, and open feeling. It represents the vital, generative energy that renews a community.
sound_symbolism: The vibrant 'me' pulses with feeling unguarded by experience - that quality of heart that hasn't yet learned to close. This emotional aliveness opens through 'a', creating endless space for becoming. The vowel hiatus captures youth's essence: feeling flowing into possibility without barrier. Two syllables hold springtime's secret - not counted in years but in the willingness to feel fully and remain open to what might be.
image_prompt: Art Nouveau watercolor in Mucha style: Unfurling spiral forms in palest gold and soft lavender suggesting growth without depicting figures. Abstract opening buds in powder blue and barely-there coral showing potential becoming actual. Transparent overlapping waves of vital force in cream and mint pushing upward and outward. Delicate organic patterns of ferns and new leaves emerging. Whispers of peach and rose capture unguarded emotional aliveness. All extremely soft pastels. Wet-on-wet technique emphasizes the flowing energy of becoming, always beginning again.
grammatical_notes: Functions as both noun (youth/young person) and adjective (youthful) without morphological change. The adjectival usage expresses the quality of being young or having youthful characteristics.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Youth carries the seeds of regenerative futures - unburdened by 'how things have always been done,' young minds imagine new possibilities. Solarpunk recognizes youth not as resources to be molded but as co-creators of tomorrow, bringing fresh perspectives essential for adaptive resilience. Their hope isn't naive but necessary.
pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Youth embodies Art Nouveau's celebration of natural growth - the unfurling fern, the opening bud, the spiral of new shells. This aesthetic sees beauty in potential becoming actual, in the vital force that pushes green shoots through concrete. Youth's energy mirrors nature's own regenerative power, always beginning again.

tags.community: vital energy that renews collective life
tags.temporal: life stage of growth and transformation
tags.aesthetic: beauty of natural growth and vital force
